reviews.review_msg: Readings are insanely long for a first-year course, multiple movies per week, and so many lecture videos. The quizzes are computer-graded. I got a question marked as wrong because one letter wasn't capitalized. If you don't remember the exact wording he used in lectures, you'll get questions wrong on the tests.

Very course heavy for a first year course. Every week was a lot of reading, an oddly specific quiz where you had to remember everything, 2 hours of lectures, then another quiz on his lectures and the movies. The projects were peer graded and the quizzes were buggy and you had to email him to fix it. It was not enjoyable, unfortunately
Overall, if you are willing to keep up and absorb material to a tea, take it, otherwise skip out

Prof assigns 2 quizzes per week, graded by a computer, all answers are case sensitive. Divides his lectures into 7 - 10 different videos, plus 1 - 2 movies per week. Assignments peer graded. Quizzes are unfair and feel like trivia for movie buffs. Work load is way too heavy for an elective. Do not take this course.

By far the worst prof I've ever had. This person is not even a prof, careless and not professional. Made the course harder for no reason. As an elective for fun he made us to 30 quizzes. Literally made my grade 2 letter grades down because he didn't let me do the missed quiz due to illness.

The laziest professor I have ever had. I would have to email him at LEAST once a week because he uses an autograder that only accepts exact punctuation/capitalization, etc. He would also straight up put incorrect answers as the answer (wish I was joking) The two main assignments are peer graded so he literally doesn't mark a single thing.

Workload was heavy for a first year intro course, definitely not what most people are expecting from a cinema class, I would imagine. Quizes are auto-graded so if you forget to capitalize or hyphenate an answer, you get it wrong. You can email him to fix this but doing so on a weekly basis becomes tiresome. Essay grades are from peer reviews.

Course is basic intro to cinema. Workload with weekly quizzes and assigned reading's plus lecture and movie viewing felt heavy, and was hard to determine which material was tested for quizzes. Some questions did not feel very fair. Project worth 40% is interesting and fun to create, my favorite part of this course. All work is peer reviewed.
He doesn't actually do any grading your whole grade is based off of peer review scores...lowest effort prof ever. Way too much work for an elective, actually insane dont take it
Worst prof ever!!! Never take his classes!!

Peter is awesome! He is probably my favorite prof I've had in my four years of university. Class had no exam but was instead based on a series of group and individual projects which were a lot of work but he was very helpful along the way. Quick at replying to emails is always a plus. Also, he does a good mix of seminar and lecture/does both well.

Peter is hands down one of the best profs I have ever had. His lectures are amazing; he is engaging and hilarious, and he is so knowledgeable about the course contents. The course materials were interesting and diverse, and the workload was reasonable and incredibly interesting. I loved this class so much!
Peter was probably the best first year professor I have ever had. I would highly recommend taking a first year course with him.

Peter is a funny, caring prof. Lecture aren't mandatory to show up to but show up because there are performances sometimes. There's a lot of reading, and it's difficult to understand the purpose/point sometimes (go to tutorial!) Grading is fair, grading criteria is clear, overall an easy course.
Really great prof!  Has interesting lectures that really make you think!  He's kinda quirky and really funny in lectures, I recommend any English class he's teaching.

Peter was my favourite prof this term. Very organized, covered a great deal of material in the short lecture time, and the course readings were very interesting. He was also very passionate about not only the content of the course, but teaching to us. Easiness, I think, depends more on your TA.

Peter is a great prof! He made class engaging and fun (even on a monday morning). He pushes you to be a better writer and actually made me enjoy English class for once. He also has great taste in reading material!
Took this class for my W-requirement. Peter makes lectures fun with jokes/DRAMA. Didn't have my tutorials with him but loved the TA I had. Overall, really enjoyable class.
Peter is an excellent prof. Well read, interesting and has great sense of humour. He awards participation and attendance. Reading material is always interesting. Very enjoyable class!

I took this class not really expecting it to be as interesting as it is! It's a play a week and each class is a student led participation which invokes discussion throughout (if the presentations are good). The final is a scene presentation from one of the plays. Peter's a great prof and this class was really interesting, good for 400-level.

Honestly took this class as a GPA booster, and it wasn't too bad. Very intriguing lectures, even though he posts the lecture slides he expands a lot on them. He was my tutorial leader as well. With him, and especially on the final be sure to master how to write a strong thesis, and you'll be set

One of my favorite profs this year! Classes are fun and interactive. He's enthusiastic and fun to listen to. His slides are online so you can download them for classes and take notes on them. Make sure you listen in class and pay attention during your tutorial.

Super nice prof! If you love quirky people who are hilarious, this is the best prof for you! Attendance isn't really mandatory for the lectures since he reads them outloud, but the way he reads them and expands on them (plus his jokes) are worth attending the lecs for. Tutorials are SUPER necessary- that's the key to a good grade. Awesome class tho
Fun teacher but no point going to lecture. Key is the tutorials. Good analysis of the works and kept class interesting
easy pass but hard to get a high mark. The key is your tutorial.

Kept class entertaining! Definately not like other professors, he can somewhat relate to us. However, he has really high expectations. Very analytic, so the essay topics were not so easy! Ended up with a B.
He tries very hard to keep the class' attention. Know the books, and its 'analyzed meaning of it'.
Overall he's an okay prof. Tries to make the material interesting. Make sure you read ahead since he jumps from one book to another pretty quickly. I find that his reading list was a bit too much.
I would really recommend this prof! He may be a little crazy at times, but it is very entertaining. Overall, if you have a good TA. you will do well in this course!

Incredibly smart guy, frequently either made up words or used ones I'd never heard of. Rewards effort and if you put the work in you'll get a great grade. I thought his marking was the fairest of any prof I've had. Entertaining and covers a lot of material, would definitely recommend him!

Peter is a really kind person who cares a lot about his students. Go to see him during his hours and he is super helpful. Yes, he definitely likes the sound of his own voice, but what prof doesn't? He's interesting and a fair marker. Highly recommended.

Self-righteous prick. Monopolizes student-led seminars, and grades unfairly. On the plus side, chooses great reading material and comments extensively on papers. If you can get past his arrogance, he's not so bad.

I really enjoyed his course, he is a fantastic prof. Take his cdn lit courses! Clear, prepared, organized, and interesting. Didn't feel particularly friendly, but u definitely feel that he's got it together. Ditto about the reading list - superb. Has a final, 2 papers, and presentation.
TRAGEDY!!! The comments appear pretty split. But keep in mind that if you decide he is a total **** (which I suspect you will), remember that he is also the English Department chair, ENJOY.

I was surprised to see him rated as 'hot' because to be honest he looks a bit creepy. Nonetheless our course's reading list was the best reading list I've ever had. His marking is harder than average and he does go off on tangents, but I would recommend taking a course with him as he chooses interesting topics and really incorporates discussion.

Peter is neither shallow nor condescending, and he doesn't play favorites--he simply rewards intelligence and hard work.  These comments about appearance, and barely-veiled homophobic criticisms like "pseudo-feminine," are just the complaints of ignorant slackers who don't want to work.  Grow up and appreciate a great prof.

Arrogant little man whose long-winded lectures are marred by a complete lack of teaching ability. Goes overtime on every lecture while having nothing interesting to say, then begrudges those who don't wish to attend. Plays favorites too: get on his good side or suffer his condescending, pseudo-femine "yeah..." everytime you try to participate.
Took this class as a hopeful GPA boost; what a disappointment. turned out to be my worst class. this man should not be teaching.

What I like to call "gameshow-style teaching". 50% of the final grade was dedicated to quizzes each trivia style question of the quiz worth 1 %of final grade!! Ridiculous! DICKinson has something to PROVE not to teach!
I loved Peter's lectures! always amusing, and the assignments and exams were clear and fair.
Worst professor I've ever had.  What a joke.
No Comments

The best prof I've ever had at SFU!  I recommend Peter to all my friends.  He is a genius -- very intelligent and funny and super well-read.  I loved every moment of his lectures and learned so much from the assignments.  Only people of low intelligence who didn't do the readings would not like his classes!
please listen to the comments below, they are very true.  it's sad that someone like this teaches at sfu, it really gives the school a bad reputation.  he's a worn stereotype, with poor ideas.

Impossibly tough marker, plays favourites constantly.  I was his buddy for the first half of the course and was getting an A, then later I stopped attending his boring lectures and suddenly he starts giving me dirty looks and my grades drop.  Beware this guy, he's very shallow.
boooo, he calls movies, talkies.  not smart, not funny, and has bad hair, but that's beside the point.

This distance ed class was pretty typical.  The last paper was quite long, but that's standard for upper-year classes.  Lots of assignments at first, with the last half of the readings just for the exam.  (But DO read everything for the exam!)  No internet required, so you can take it from anywhere.
painful lectures, in which it became increasingly obvious how clueless this guy actually is....avoid his classes at all cost, you have been warned
Best english class ever. Very enthusiastic teacher and if you pay attention in lectures, the class is a breeze

His class would be seen as nothing more than pop culture fodder if it wasn't for his remarkable attention to detail, and the fact he's extremely well read.  Peter has a lengthy theoretical library committed to memory which allows him to articulate on very 'trendy' works in a scholarly manner.

Peter is THE best English prof I've had at SFU. He is incredibly intelligent, motivated, and well-articulated. I actually enjoyed his class. The only downside is that he expects a lot from his students, but I guess that is to be expected w/ a 300 & 400-level classes.
Absolutely one of the best profs I've ever had. Funny, interesting, creative, and very, very smart. He can make anything sound interesting and he sets a very open class environment.
Lectures are informative and exciting ... vested interest in course material ... gives feedback on areas needing improvement ... a great prof!
very strict marking scheme.

The course wasn't as interesting as I thought it to be but the guy really loves what he is teaching. One BAD thing about this course is there is way too much work for a 100 level intro english course. It was kinda rough having him as a tutorial leader.
an easy course overall, but the prof is not very helpful and knowing the material in the textbook well does not guarantee high marks!!
best prof I had at SFU
very very good prof! makes english a fun subject to learn!
Lectures are dynamic and interesting. Course materials are not overly difficult. This is probably the best course to get an ENGL credit. He is one of the best professors in SFU.
dickinson was great! easy to understand guy, which is hard to find in university. a great class thanks to a great proff! =)
Really nice guy...seemed very passionate about literary drama. His dynamic teaching style and outgoing personality make the course substance way more interesting.
Great prof, very understanding, and cute.  Great lectures and obvious interest in course material.
He is one of the best profs I've ever had - dynamic, funny and very intelligent.  Awesome lecturer!

He's passionate about the material he is teaching.  He tries to stir up class discussion even though it is a lecture environment. Very good at analyzing the play in relating it to other social issues!!!!
Funny prof. Liberated in expression and is clear in his lectures. It makes us students who took the course appreciate the plays that we study even better.

It's so great to have a prof who just oozes creativity. He is the best person to have a 9:30 class with 'cause it puts you in a good mood all day long. I'm always tempted to declare my undying love for him!
Barely literate. Pretentious. Good to look at.
very good prof...show's a lot of enthusiam
